violations.description: Preventing contamination from equipment, utensils, and wiping cloths. — --Cloth for wiping food spills was not stored in sanitizing solution (at proper concentration) between uses. -Kitchen did not have sani bucket made/ready to use. -Server sani buckets had been made with 1/2 cap bleach and soap, found with 0ppm Chlorine at time of inspection.

Cooling of potentially hazardous food. — --Cooked potentially hazardous food was not cooled properly from 140F to 70F or below within two hours. -Philly cheesesteak soup taken off heat at approximately 1:30pm found at 3:30 pm with edge temperature of 41F and center temperature of 140F. Had staff stir product, temperature was 80F throughout. *Directed to dispose of product.
Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not covered or inverted during storage. -Dishes/containers at top shelf above make line are not inverted or covered.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. -Back up make line products found in oven with temperatures of 126F to 138F. *Had staff return taco meat (126F) and beef (132F) to oven to reheat and recommended increasing temperature in oven used for hot hold to ensure foods are maintained at 140F or higher.--Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Whole baked potatoes had been removed from refigeration and held at room temperature for approximately one hour during service (58F). *Directed to return to refrigeration. -South cold line of buffet found with temperature range of 45F-48F. *Recommended icing product. -Cake with peanut butter Cream cheese frosting being served from room temperature at service line. *Directed to refrigerate item.
Sanitizing solution testing devices. — --Test strip or kit that accurately measures the sanitizing concentration was not provided. -Quat test strips are not available for Quat sanitizer buckets.

Employee dressing areas and lockers. — --employees' clothing/possessions were in an area where contamination of food, equipment, utensils, linens or single-service items may occur. -Several coats/jackets hung on 3 different corners of clean storage racks.
General employee cleanliness. — --Food employee did not wash hands as necessary. -Cooks changed gloves several times but did not handwash.
General employee practices. — --Employee failed to maintain a high degree of personal cleanliness or observe proper hygienic practices. -Employee wiped knife on apron between uses.

Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Equipment that dispenses liquid/food/ice in unpackaged form was not cleaned properly and/or as frequently as required. -Black/yellow debris along bottom edge of interior panel of ice machine. -Debris at interior surfaces of juice dispenser.

Use of poisonous and toxic materials. — --Poisonous or toxic material was not used and/or applied according to manufacturer's label. -Kitchen Sani Bucket found with greater than 500ppm Quat solution. *Directed staff to manually dilute solutions until dispenser is fixed. -Server sani buckets made with dish soap and chlorine.

Food display and service. — --Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 140F or above during display or service. -Baked fish at hot hold (far left) found with temperature of 118F-140F. *Reheated at oven and then returned to hot well.--Potentially hazardous food was not kept at an internal temperature of 41F or below during display or service. -Batter at waffle maker found with a temperature of 63F. (Disposed of.) -Whipped butter taken out at ~10am found with temp of 54-71F-returned to walk in cooler. -Whipped butter at cook line found at 72F. -Pooled eggs, pancake batter and hashbrowns in refrigerator found at 54-63F. (Had been out at cookline during breakfast service.) -Coleslaw in ice bath at server station found with temp of 43F-50F. *Recommended moving away from heat lamp and adding water to ice so slurry surrounds product better.
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, labeling, use (3 pts deducted)
Handwashing, good hygienic practices (5 pts deducted)
Clean clothes, hair restraints, fingernail maint., jewelry, eating/drinking (1 pt deducted)
Hot holding and cold holding temperatures maintained, unwrapped food not reserved (5 pts deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
Clean dry location, protected (1 pt deducted)
Proper storage, no-reuse (1 pt deducted)
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Storage, properly handled (1 pt deducted)
Available, adequate capacity, able to maintain proper temperature (4 pts deducted)
Method, temperature and time (5 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (2 pts deducted)
